description: POST /v1/tasks/:id/log — a source uplink (source OR owner, since owner is a source-superset, P0-1; NOT viewer). Best-effort uplink of task log lines, appended to the per-task 100-line ring buffer in task_logs (v1-architecture.md §1.2.2, §3). NON-folded: does not advance space_revision, emits no delta, not on the device_seq path. Replaces the legacy /v2/ingest task.log passthrough. Paired with the GET read below.

post_lines.note: Authenticated as the OWNER device dev_own_8f2a1c (owner_token from create-space.json) — the same device that uplinks events in events-post-ack.json. owner is a source-superset (P0-1), so it may POST log lines just as a `source` device may; a `viewer` token would get 403 (see error_403_viewer_cannot_post). This is a source uplink, the mirror image of POST /v1/events (v1-architecture.md §3).

read_back_get.note: GET is viewer/owner (the viewer token from join.json here); the ring buffer holds at most the last 100 lines (TASK_LOG_WINDOW), oldest first.

lines: viewer should not be able to write this

error_403_viewer_cannot_post.note: A viewer token resolves fine but the viewer role is not permitted to POST log lines — only source (and owner, as a source-superset) may uplink.
